title: rhus fraxinifolia
intro: Rhus fraxinifolia, also known as the man root or fringe tree, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rhus fraxinifolia include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a treatment for wounds and snake bites. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made by drying the roots and leaves of the plant.
